definition: The court dismissed the charge, ending the case on that charge without a conviction.
The prosecution withdrew the charge before a verdict or plea, ending the case on that charge.
The defendant entered a plea of guilty or no contest, resulting in a conviction on the charge.
A judge or jury found the defendant guilty after a trial, resulting in a conviction on the charge.
A judge or jury found the defendant not guilty after a trial.
The charge was handled through Accelerated Rehabilitative Disposition, a Pennsylvania pretrial program that can end in dismissal when its requirements are completed.
The charge was handled through a diversion program other than ARD, in which the defendant completes program requirements instead of proceeding to trial or sentencing.
The recorded disposition does not fit any of the defined outcome categories.
The disposition could not be determined from the available court records.
